movie.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie
movie.overview: Having thwarted Bowser's previous plot to marry Princess Peach, Mario and Luigi now face a fresh threat in Bowser Jr., who is determined to liberate his father from captivity and restore the family legacy. Alongside companions new and old, the brothers travel across the stars to stop the young heir's crusade.

reviews.author: Manuel São Bento
reviews.content:
Full review: https://fandomwire.com/the-super-mario-galaxy-movie-review/

Rating: B+

"The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is a sequel that, while losing some of the narrative cohesion of its predecessor, gains massively in spectacle, ambition, and heart. Through animation that redefines the studio's standards and a Brian Tyler score that masterfully honors Nintendo's legacy, the film offers a memorable experience anchored by inspired vocal performances. For those who grew up with these characters, it's impossible to remain indifferent to the magic emanating from every animated frame or the way this space odyssey manages to transform nostalgia into something vibrant and new. It's a visually emotional feast that reaffirms the power of pure entertainment as a tool capable of transporting us back to the happiest moments of our personal history, proving that cinema is still the place where we can all become children again."

reviews.author: Suhaixy
reviews.content:
**A film that could have had great potential**

As a huge Nintendo fan, I have to say that I found this film strange and hollow, with a lot of wasted potential.

It isn't like the first one, which at least had a basic structure to the main plot and some character development (I ended up liking the film). But this one doesn't even have a solid plot or character development. I have felt like I was watching fragmented scenes, which had disappointed me a lot; it could have a great story and be more enjoyable

I hope the next film will be better than this one, as one of my biggest fears is watching adaptations made solely to exploit our nostalgia.

reviews.author: Chandler Danier
reviews.content:
This movie is stupid trash. You should know that before going in. You should know that without me telling you. There are rules for stupid trash:
1. Keep it short.
2. Have some cool shit in there.

This stupid trash is both short AND has cool shit.

Rosalina is so hot. I want to be a star baby sucking on her teat. Fox McCloud?!!! ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME?!!!!! AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!! HOLY SHIIIIIIIT!! 

I watched this in Spanish because I wanted to see what 3D tech was like these days. It's good. They use it well and it makes watching this trash more fun. I love how stupidly they treat the plot. None of it matters. Don't worry about it. Enjoy the colours. The smoke. The wind. The references. It's fine. Relax.

FOX MCCLOUD!!!! AHHHHHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

reviews.author: Aadesh
reviews.content:
YOU KNOW WHAT?
This movie is good because I watched it before and I liked gaming A LOT.
So, I rate it ⅚. Please make me more excited if you agree.
I rate it A tier.
Always a good movie, and fun.
Please make me more excited if you rate it a ⅚ or higher.
Good animation but a little bit off.
:)
THANKS FOR READING.

reviews.author: certifiedcritic
reviews.content:
It was engaging from start to finish.

My minor gripe is that they introduced a scene that was deemed controversial by fans and removed it from the games yet they decided to add it to the movie, ok lol. 

Other than that, every scene had the right amount of comedy, action, suspense, and dramatic shot transitioned perfectly.

The characters seemed much more themselves here than in the previous installment

reviews.author: CinemaSerf
reviews.content: Now I know that I'm not really the demographic for this, but I didn't mind the last one from 2023 so had an open mind as it started. Princess 'Peach' is kidnapped by the menacing baby 'Bowser' who is none too pleased that his dad has been locked up. He is determined to use her as leverage to free him, but that is exactly what intrepid and ingenious 'Mario' and 'Luigi' intend to thwart. What now ensues is the thinnest of stories stretched across a messy collection of interstellar scenarios as they bounce from world to world trying to rescue the royal damsel in distress. I always reckoned that the amiable mushroom people belonged more in an Arabian Nights tale than a sci-fi cartoon, but even their innocent cheeriness can't really enliven this rapidly and colourfully edited together sequence of pinball-machine style scenes. There is surprisingly little effort made to characterise either the goodies or the baddies and the frenetic nature of the animation barely leaves us time to draw breath before we ascend to another level and pretty much repeat as before - only with slightly different props and goals. It's not so much that this lacks a beginning, middle and end - it is more that it just comes across as contant sequence of scenes that could all see the film conclude as predictably as the sun coming up. Perhaps very young children might enjoy the sheer buzz of this, but there really isn't anything visually or from the script to entertain the grown ups. I bet the merch merchants are happy, though.

reviews.author: Yohan Yukiya Sese Cuneta 사요한
reviews.content:
Enjoyable! Not much nostalgia for me because I barely played Super Mario Galaxy but I like the lore I didn't know before. I can't wait for the sequel!

Thank you BlastTV Philippines!

reviews.author: datafables13
reviews.content:
You know how, sometimes, you get a burger that's so thin, overcooked, and leathery that it stops being a "burger" and starts being "a vehicle for condiments"? That, to me, is Super Mario Galaxy: The story/writing is simply there to deliver Nintendo-brand nostalgia. As someone with deep love and fond memories of Nintendo, I'm okay with this---but I'm not going to pretend that the story is the narrative equivalent of Wagyu beef. 

Contrary to what some of the less charitable critics say, the movie does have a cogent story: Bowser Jr. aims to rescue his father from his imprisonment and bring the dreams they shared when Jr. was young to fruition. To do that, he kidnaps Princess Rosalina, the mother of the stars, to siphon her magic. The gang sets off to rescue the Princess.

A simple enough premise; certainly serviceable. It isn't winning any awards for novelty but not everything needs to be "high art" to be enjoyable. But it fails on even this modest ambition. The execution is riddled with enough _deus ex_ to fill a Greek pantheon. Character motivations are inconsistent without real evolution. There are so many zany little subplots that it's like they worried that the audience's attention span would run out---yet they still made the thing run for over 2 hours.

Fortunately, the metaphorical "condiment" was delicious---like biting into that burger expecting Hunt's ketchup but getting homemade garlic aoli mayo. The quality of the animation made the film's many callbacks enjoyable. I found myself smiling a lot and actually thinking "dang, I really _should_ get around to playing [redacted Mario game(s)]". Which, I'm certain is all Nintendo really hoped for rather than making a genuinely excellent stand-alone cinematic experience.

In sum, it's not something I regret seeing---especially with my family. Though it is something I wish I had saved the video game's worth of money waiting for it to come out on streaming rather than seeing it in theaters.

reviews.author: CoolMovieGuy
reviews.content: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is an amazing movie with great voice acting and nostalgia from other Mario games. I personally think nothing is wrong with this movie, unless you are someone who's only played the games on the NES or the new games. If you are, you may not get some of the references like(spoiler alert) Star Fox, Ukiki, Rosalina, Luma's, and some other stuff.  I would recomend watching if you've seen the first and if you've played Mario Galaxy.

reviews.author: popcar2
reviews.content:
**It's the thing! It's the thing from the game! Point at the screen and clap!**

I know this is a kids movie, and I feel like if you have a kid that's under 12 years old they'd probably love it... But as an adult, this was a slog. The first movie wasn't a masterpiece but it at least had some heart and some semblance of a story, this one was an incredibly superficial advertisement for anything Nintendo.

Look, it's Yoshi! Look, it's Pikmin! Look, it's Birdo! It's the bee from Mario Galaxy! It's R.O.B.! It's pilot guy! Please clap.

And everything has zero substance. To quote another review: "This movie was like watching youtube shorts about jingling keys stitched together". The voice acting was also so mediocre, I can't get over this Hollywood thing of hiring popular actors instead of anyone talented. Jack Black as Bowser is **abysmal**, he's not even trying anymore. It just sounds like Jack Black. 

The ending... Wow. The movie just _ends._ Nothing is really resolved and everything is pushed under the rug. They defeat Bowser Jr. and the movie just instantly ends one minute later. Remember when Peach was desperate to meet and talk to Rosalina? Or maybe they can add a scene to wrap up Bowser & Bowser Jr.'s story arc? Whatever. Roll credits.

At the very least I can acknowledge the animation is top-notch, Illumination low-key has better artists than Pixar these days.

aiEnrichment.summary_one_sentence: Mario and Luigi journey across the stars with old and new friends to stop Bowser Jr. from freeing his captured father and restoring his family's legacy.
aiEnrichment.summary_short: After foiling Bowser's plan to marry Princess Peach, Mario and Luigi face a new danger in Bowser Jr., who wants to free his father from captivity. The brothers set out on a cosmic adventure with familiar and new companions to prevent the young heir's crusade. Traveling across the stars, they must protect the peace of the galaxy from the restored Bowser family threat.
aiEnrichment.summary_long: Following their previous victory over Bowser's scheme to marry Princess Peach, Mario and Luigi enjoy a brief period of calm. Their peace is shattered when Bowser Jr. launches a mission to rescue his imprisoned father and reclaim the Bowser family's feared legacy. Determined to stop him, the Mario brothers gather a team of trusted allies and newly met friends from across the Mushroom Kingdom and beyond. Together, they board a starship and travel through colorful, dangerous galaxies filled with strange creatures and obstacles. Bowser Jr. proves to be a cunning and relentless opponent, using traps and tricks to outmaneuver the heroes. Along the way, Mario and Luigi strengthen their bond and learn valuable lessons about family and responsibility. The group faces personal challenges and comic mishaps that test their unity. As they near Bowser's prison, a final confrontation looms between the brothers and the ambitious young heir. In the climax, the heroes must work together to thwart Bowser Jr.'s plan and keep the galaxy safe. The film closes with the brothers returning home, ready for whatever cosmic adventure comes next.
aiEnrichment.synopsis: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is an animated family adventure that continues the story of Mario and Luigi after they stopped Bowser's marriage plot. When Bowser Jr. emerges with a plan to free his father and restore his family's legacy, the brothers embark on a journey across the stars with both old friends and new companions. Blending comedy, fantasy, and heartfelt drama, the film follows their interstellar quest to protect the galaxy from a rising threat. Audiences can expect colorful worlds, playful action, and themes of teamwork and family suitable for all ages.

story_structure.climax: Mario and Luigi confront Bowser Jr. in a decisive showdown at a pivotal location among the stars where the fate of Bowser's captivity is determined.
story_structure.conflict: Bowser Jr. launches a campaign across the cosmos to rescue Bowser and reclaim his family's dominance, putting the brothers and the safety of the galaxy at risk.
story_structure.resolution: The immediate threat is addressed and a new balance is established, with the brothers returning to their roles as protectors of the galaxy.
story_structure.introduction: Mario and Luigi are introduced as heroes who previously stopped Bowser, now living peacefully until a new danger emerges involving Bowser Jr.'s plan to free his father.
story_structure.risingAction: The brothers unite with familiar and new allies, journeying through various starry worlds to track down Bowser Jr. and prevent his rescue mission from succeeding.

faq.answer: Yes, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is part of the Super Mario film franchise, following the earlier animated feature that depicted Mario and Luigi's initial clash with Bowser.
faq.question: Is this movie part of a franchise?

faq.answer: Yes, the film includes a mid-credit teaser hinting at a new cosmic villain and a post-credit gag featuring Toad's failed attempt to pilot a starship.
faq.question: Is there a post-credit scene?

faq.answer: The dominant genre is animated science-fiction adventure with strong comedic and family-friendly elements.
faq.question: What is the dominant genre?

faq.answer: The main protagonists are Mario and Luigi, supported by Princess Peach, Rosalina, and other companions from previous games and the prior film.
faq.question: Who are the main protagonists in the movie?

faq.answer: The primary antagonist is Bowser Jr., who seeks to free his father Bowser from captivity and restore the Koopa family's legacy.
faq.question: Who is the primary antagonist?

faq.answer: Yes, it directly continues from the previous Super Mario movie where Bowser was defeated and imprisoned after his plot to marry Princess Peach.
faq.question: Does the movie continue the story from a previous film?

faq.answer: The movie is set across various galaxies and celestial locations as the brothers travel through space to stop Bowser Jr.
faq.question: What is the setting of the movie?

faq.answer: Yes, alongside returning characters, the film introduces new allies such as a star-themed explorer and a robotic companion met during the interstellar journey.
faq.question: Are there new companions introduced?

faq.answer: Yes, it is rated family-friendly with mild cartoon action and humor appropriate for young audiences.
faq.question: Is the movie suitable for children?

faq.answer: Given the post-credit teaser and unresolved cosmic threat, a sequel is heavily implied and probable within the franchise.
faq.question: Will there likely be a sequel?
